zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Mason於1510年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾伍德福特時，她的父親William Mason II22歲，母親Elizabeth Leighton31歲。她於1526年與Thomas de Totehyll在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1590年享年80歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Mason于1510年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾伍德福特时，她的父亲William Mason II22岁，母亲Elizabeth Leighton31岁。她于1526年与Thomas de Totehyll在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1590年享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mason
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English masoun ‘mason, stone worker, builder in stone’ (Old Central French maçon, masson). A Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun) gives rise to Machin and its variants. See also Mayson . Stonemasonry was a hugely important craft in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Masson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Lucien, Alain, Henri, Pierre, Yves, Agathe, Aime, Cecile, Clovis, Francois, Francoise.

variants.name: Machin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Juan, Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Mario, Miguel, Pedro, Blanca, Diego, Luis, Ramon.
English: variant of   Machen  . This is the more common form of the name in Britain and Ireland.
Spanish (Machín): probably a nickname from machín ‘boor, lout’, often applied to a blacksmith's apprentice.

variants.name: Macon
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places so called, e.g. in Saône-et-Loire.
French: from the old oblique form of the ancient Germanic personal name Mako, a variant of Mago, a short form of compound names formed with maht ‘strength, power’ as the first element.
French (Maçon): occupational name for a mason, (Old) French maçon.

variants.name: Machen
meanings: English: variant of Mason , from the Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Mason was born in 1510, in Woodford, Northamptonshire, England, her father, William Mason II, was 22 and her mother, Elizabeth Leighton, was 31. She married Thomas de Totehyll in 1526, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1590, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Mason est née en 1510, à Woodford, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, William Mason II, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Leighton, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas de Totehyll en 1526, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1590, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Mason 1510 in Woodford, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Mason II 22 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Leighton 31. Sie hat 1526 in England Thomas de Totehyll geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1590 im Alter von 80 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Mason è nata il nel 1510 nel Woodford, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, William Mason II, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Leighton, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Thomas de Totehyll nel 1526, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1590, all'età di 80 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mason(이)가 1510년 영국 노르섬프토실르 우드퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Mason II(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Leighton(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1526년 영국에서 Thomas de Totehyll(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1590년 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Mason родилась в 1510 г. в местности вудфорд, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Mason II было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Leighton, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas de Totehyll в 1526 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1590 г. в возрасте 80 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Mason nació en 1510, en Woodford, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Mason II, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Leighton, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Thomas de Totehyll en 1526, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1590, a la edad de 80 años.
